mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative equity returns within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized demand growth across production and consumption channels, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and causing immediate negative market reactions over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to positive during tightening periods or 5-day forward returns show no significant deviation from zero (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation months or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand without slack, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of observations or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts directly increase demand for construction materials, appliances, and financial services, expanding corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or housing starts show zero correlation with 60-day forward equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during a post-shock recovery phase indicates clearing supply chain constraints and margin expansion, triggering rapid positive equity repricing within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to generate positive returns in >60% of recovery-phase observations or loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shifts.
